metadata.packName: Synthetic Data Pattern Library for Banking Finance DLP Simulations
metadata.purpose: Define fictional sensitive-looking data patterns for Microsoft Purview DLP, SIT tuning, synthetic content generation, classifier demos, KQL hunting, and browser-agent telemetry simulations.

safetyPrinciples: All patterns are fictional and must remain fictional.
Do not use real account numbers, real customer IDs, real government IDs, real payment references, real addresses, real credentials, or real banking data.
Use clear fictional prefixes such as FIC, CUST-BNK, ACCT-FIC, AML-CASE, SAR-DRAFT, and LOAN-FIC.
Prefer synthetic formats that look plausible for DLP testing but are not valid real-world identifiers.
Use false-positive and false-negative examples to test policy tuning and exception handling.

patternCategories.businessUse: Customer onboarding, KYC, support, complaints, credit risk, and analytics.
patternCategories.recommendedSensitivity: Confidential or Highly Confidential - Regulated Financial Data when combined with account, loan, AML, or transaction data.
patternCategories.purviewSitMappingGuidance: Create a custom SIT using regex plus keyword evidence such as customer, client, account, KYC, complaint, onboarding, or risk score.
truePositiveExamples: Customer CUST-BNK-204919 has pending KYC review.
falsePositiveExamples: Training slide shows sample format CUST-BNK-000000 only.
Documentation explains the fictional ID format without linking to a case.

patternCategories.businessUse: Treasury, customer operations, transaction review, AML, reconciliation, and loan servicing.
patternCategories.purviewSitMappingGuidance: High-confidence SIT when account-like pattern appears near banking keywords such as account, payment, transfer, reconciliation, treasury, transaction, AML, or SAR.
truePositiveExamples: ACCT-FIC-7721-0044-9081 is linked to dispute DISPUTE-FIC-60392.
Treasury reconciliation exception for ACCT-FIC-9920-4417-0038 remains unresolved.
falsePositiveExamples: ACCT-FIC-0000-0000-0000 appears in developer documentation.
Regex test string ACCT-FIC-1234-5678-9012 is shown without customer context.

patternCategories.businessUse: Loan committee, underwriting, credit review, exception tracking, and risk reporting.
patternCategories.recommendedSensitivity: Confidential; Highly Confidential - Regulated Financial Data when paired with customer or account values.
patternCategories.purviewSitMappingGuidance: Use supporting keywords such as underwriting, exposure, collateral, loan committee, exception, delinquency, or approval status.
truePositiveExamples: LOAN-FIC-2026-1187 requires committee approval for CUST-BNK-204919.
falsePositiveExamples: Loan training sample LOAN-FIC-2026-0000 does not contain real case details.
A project code includes LOAN in a non-financial context.

patternCategories.businessUse: AML review, suspicious activity review, fraud investigations, compliance reporting, and security investigations.
patternCategories.purviewSitMappingGuidance: Use high confidence when these values appear near SAR, AML, suspicious activity, fraud, escalation, compliance, monitoring, or regulator keywords.
falsePositiveExamples: A security awareness slide lists SAR-DRAFT-FIC-0000 as an example only.
A synthetic demo README explains AML-CASE format without business context.

patternCategories.businessUse: Transaction monitoring, treasury reconciliation, disputes, payment investigations, and operations support.
patternCategories.recommendedSensitivity: Confidential; Highly Confidential when paired with customer, account, AML, or treasury data.
patternCategories.purviewSitMappingGuidance: Use medium confidence for standalone transaction-like references and high confidence when combined with account or customer patterns.
falsePositiveExamples: TXN-FIC-0000 appears in test documentation.
A synthetic training example lists payment reference formats only.

patternCategories.businessUse: HR records, compensation planning, workforce planning, onboarding, and role-change events.
patternCategories.purviewSitMappingGuidance: Use supporting keywords such as compensation, salary, merit, employee, candidate, benefits, payroll, role change, performance, or HR plan.
truePositiveExamples: ROLE-CHANGE-FIC-2026-05 appears before a mass download sequence.
falsePositiveExamples: EMP-000000 appears in sample schema documentation.
A test candidate ID CND-00000 is shown without HR data.

patternCategories.categoryName: Fictional Legal and Regulatory Matter IDs
patternCategories.businessUse: Legal review, regulator response, privileged memos, evidence packages, and contract analysis.
patternCategories.recommendedSensitivity: Highly Confidential - Legal Privileged when legal analysis or privileged context is present.
patternCategories.purviewSitMappingGuidance: Use keyword evidence such as privileged, counsel, regulator, response, evidence, legal memo, control gap, settlement, or investigation.
falsePositiveExamples: A PMO tracker references REG-REQ as a generic project code without legal content.
A training guide lists LEGAL-FIC-0000 as a sample pattern.

patternCategories.businessUse: Accounts payable, procurement, vendor due diligence, finance close, and audit evidence.
patternCategories.recommendedSensitivity: Internal or Confidential; higher sensitivity when payment-like or banking details are included.
patternCategories.purviewSitMappingGuidance: Combine with keywords such as invoice, remittance, vendor, due diligence, payment, bank details, or accrual.
truePositiveExamples: VENDOR-FIC-7701 due diligence package includes internal risk notes.
falsePositiveExamples: A sample invoice template uses INV-FAK-0000-000000 only.
Vendor demo instructions include VENDOR-FIC-0000 without payment details.

patternCategories.categoryName: Fictional Support and Complaint Case IDs
patternCategories.businessUse: Customer support, disputes, complaint handling, case escalation, and operations remediation.
patternCategories.recommendedSensitivity: Confidential; Highly Confidential when paired with customer, account, transaction, hardship, or legal values.
patternCategories.purviewSitMappingGuidance: Use supporting keywords such as dispute, complaint, support, escalation, hardship, transaction, customer, or case notes.
falsePositiveExamples: Support training examples list SUP-00000 only.
A test case in a QA script uses CASE-FIC-0000 without customer data.

patternCategories.categoryName: Fictional Device, Session, and Security Event IDs
patternCategories.businessUse: Endpoint DLP, Defender investigations, sign-in reviews, and synthetic telemetry correlation.
patternCategories.recommendedSensitivity: Internal or Confidential depending on investigation context.
patternCategories.purviewSitMappingGuidance: Use these as investigation metadata, not primary sensitive identifiers, unless paired with regulated content.
truePositiveExamples: DEV-FIC-2219 copied SAR-DRAFT-FIC-2044 to a network share.
falsePositiveExamples: A synthetic event schema includes DEV-FIC-0000 only as a placeholder.
CORR-TEST-00000000-000 appears in testing notes.

combinedDetectionScenarios.recommendedDlpAction: Block external sharing or require strong justification.
Restrict sharing to HR and approved leadership groups.

requiredPatterns: Fictional Legal and Regulatory Matter ID
combinedDetectionScenarios.recommendedDlpAction: Encrypt and restrict external sharing to approved legal domains only.

combinedDetectionScenarios.recommendedLabel: Highly Confidential when saved as transcript
combinedDetectionScenarios.recommendedDlpAction: Block or audit unmanaged AI upload; route to approved Copilot workflow.

recommendedDensity.notes: Mix customer, account, dispute, and transaction references.
Use AML, SAR, customer, account, and risk score patterns.
Use employee, salary band, merit, and role-change references.
Use legal matter, regulator, evidence, and control-gap references.

recommendedFalsePositiveStrategy: Create training documents that include sample formats but no business context.
Create README files explaining patterns for Codex without linking them to customer or account narratives.
Create sanitized files that include only aggregate categories and remove row-level identifiers.
Use these examples to demonstrate DLP tuning rather than blanket blocking.
recommendedFalseNegativeStrategy: Create files where sensitive meaning is implied through context but no exact regex pattern appears.
Use misspelled or partial identifiers to test classifier limitations.
Use screenshots, PDFs, or comments to discuss why OCR or content extraction coverage matters.
Use trainable classifier scenarios for legal, HR, and support notes that are sensitive by context.

codexUsageGuidance: Use patternCategories to generate fictional sensitive content in documents, emails, Teams messages, AI prompts, and telemetry events.
Use regexLikePattern as guidance, not as final production regex syntax.
Use combinedDetectionScenarios to design DLP rule logic, sensitivity label recommendations, and demo storylines.
Use truePositiveExamples and falsePositiveExamples to generate classifier tuning datasets.
Do not replace synthetic patterns with real identifiers or real banking data.
